name: Detective
description: A mystery subgenre centered on a dedicated investigator whose method — logic, observation, or persistence — drives the unraveling of a case.

nodes.label: Detective Promise
nodes.definition: The audience is promised the experience of watching a brilliant or dedicated investigator crack a case through method and insight — the satisfaction of an ordered mind working against chaos, culminating in a revelation that feels both surprising and inevitable.
entry_conditions: Story is positioned as a detective narrative
exit_conditions: Core constraints are established that fulfill this promise
typical_variants: Classic genius sleuth
Gritty hardboiled investigator
Police procedural
Amateur consulting detective
failure_modes: Investigator is passive — solution is handed over rather than earned
Method is absent — detective simply gets lucky
Case is trivial and unworthy of narrative weight
signals_in_text: Opening establishes a case that demands investigation
Detective's presence immediately signals the application of method to mystery
Reader/viewer is invited to observe and deduce alongside the investigator

nodes.label: Dedicated Investigator as Central Character
nodes.definition: A specific, named investigator must function as the story's central consciousness and driving force — the one whose choices, observations, and deductions move the case forward. Supporting characters may assist, but the investigator owns the case.
exit_conditions: A single investigator is identifiable as the protagonist and primary problem-solver
typical_variants: Lone genius detective
Detective with loyal partner
Detective as leader of a team
Investigator embedded in institutional structure
failure_modes: Case is solved by committee with no clear lead
Investigator is incidental — plot advances through unrelated characters
Detective role is split among too many characters to maintain focus
signals_in_text: Scenes are anchored to the investigator's perspective or actions
Case briefings and discoveries are filtered through the investigator
Other characters defer to or challenge the investigator specifically

nodes.label: Investigator's Method Must Be Visible and Consistent
nodes.definition: The detective's approach to solving the case — whether pure logic, acute observation, persistent procedure, gut intuition, or social manipulation — must be legible to the audience and applied consistently. The method defines the detective and must be demonstrated, not merely asserted.
exit_conditions: Detective's method is demonstrated in at least one early scene and applied consistently throughout
typical_variants: Deductive reasoning made explicit through monologue or dialogue
Physical observation of overlooked details
Systematic procedural elimination
Reading people through behavioral tells
Intuitive leaps explained in retrospect
failure_modes: Detective solves problems without showing reasoning — solution appears from nowhere
Method changes arbitrarily based on plot convenience
Method is named but never demonstrated
signals_in_text: Detective explains reasoning to a partner or observer
Scenes show the detective noticing what others miss
Method becomes a character trait that creates both advantages and blind spots

nodes.label: The Case Must Be Genuinely Challenging
nodes.definition: The mystery must present a real intellectual and procedural challenge — one that is genuinely difficult, contains genuine misdirection, and requires the full application of the detective's skill and effort to solve. A case that resolves too easily robs the genre of its core satisfaction.
exit_conditions: The case has presented at least one genuine obstacle, red herring, or complexity that the detective must work to overcome
typical_variants: Impossibly complex evidence trail
Multiple viable suspects with genuine motives
Deliberate obfuscation by a clever antagonist
Case that defies conventional explanation until solved
failure_modes: Case is too simple — solved in the first act without meaningful effort
Suspects are obviously guilty from the start
No meaningful misdirection — straight line from crime to culprit
signals_in_text: Detective encounters setbacks or dead ends
Initial conclusions prove wrong and must be revised
Audience is genuinely uncertain about the solution until the reveal

nodes.label: Solution Must Come from the Detective's Work
nodes.definition: The case's resolution must be directly caused by the detective's investigation — their observations, deductions, interviews, or persistence must provide the essential link that closes the case. Coincidental resolution, unsolicited confession, or deus ex machina undercuts the genre contract.
exit_conditions: The final solution is traceable back to specific investigative actions taken by the detective
typical_variants: Solution through logical synthesis of gathered evidence
Solution through breaking down a suspect's resistance
Solution through physical discovery of concealed evidence
Solution through testing and eliminating alternative hypotheses
failure_modes: Suspect confesses spontaneously without detective pressure
Evidence materializes without investigative effort
Solution is provided by another character, not the detective
Coincidental event resolves the case
signals_in_text: Detective can trace each step of their reasoning chain
Final revelation connects directly to earlier scenes of investigation
Other characters recognize the detective's work as the cause of resolution

nodes.label: Classic / Golden Age Detective
nodes.definition: The detective story as intellectual puzzle — a genius or highly gifted sleuth applies rigorous logical method to a well-constructed mystery, often in a contained social world, with the emphasis on the elegance of the puzzle and the brilliance of its solution rather than on physical danger or moral ambiguity.
entry_conditions: Emphasis on puzzle elegance and logical method over danger or grit
exit_conditions: Solution is delivered through a formal revelation scene demonstrating the full chain of deduction
typical_variants: Country house murder mystery
Locked-room puzzle
Eccentric genius investigator
Amateur detective with superior observational gifts
failure_modes: Puzzle is inelegant — solution requires information the audience couldn't have accessed
Detective is smug without justification — genius asserted but not demonstrated
Stakes feel too low to generate genuine engagement
signals_in_text: Carefully planted clues visible in retrospect
Detective delivers formal solution presentation
Social world is a closed system of suspects
Tone is controlled, mannered, or playful despite serious crime

nodes.label: Hardboiled Private Eye
nodes.definition: The detective operates in a corrupt, dangerous world where moral compromise is unavoidable and the investigator's personal code — not institutional authority — is the only reliable compass. Danger, violence, and moral complexity replace puzzle elegance as the genre's primary texture.
entry_conditions: World is morally corrupt and physically dangerous
Investigator operates outside or at the margins of institutional authority
exit_conditions: Resolution involves the detective confronting the corruption of the world, not merely solving a puzzle
typical_variants: Private eye in a corrupt city
Former cop who left the force over principles
Investigator whose personal life is entangled with the case
Detective who wins the case but loses something personally
failure_modes: World is corrupt in name only — no real moral compromise is required
Detective's personal code is never tested
Danger is present but doesn't alter the detective's fundamental safety
signals_in_text: First-person narration with a cynical, weary voice
Physical danger to the detective is real and recurring
Clients are morally compromised or dishonest
World resists clean resolution

nodes.label: Police Detective
nodes.definition: The investigator operates within an institutional structure — police department, intelligence agency, forensic unit — and the genre's texture includes procedural realism, bureaucratic constraint, teamwork, and the tension between institutional rules and individual pursuit of truth.
entry_conditions: Investigator operates within a formal law-enforcement or investigative institution
exit_conditions: Resolution involves both solving the case and navigating institutional context
typical_variants: Homicide detective in a precinct
FBI/federal investigator
Forensic specialist solving cases through science
Detective who clashes with institutional hierarchy over methods
failure_modes: Institution is merely decorative — procedural elements don't affect the story
Teamwork is absent — solo investigation with police label only
Institutional constraints create no meaningful tension
signals_in_text: Scenes involve institutional processes — warrants, chain of command, forensic protocols
Team dynamics and hierarchy affect decisions
Detective must justify methods to superiors or partners
Institutional resources both help and constrain the investigation

nodes.label: Amateur / Consulting Detective
nodes.definition: The investigator lacks official authority and operates from an outsider position — brought in by necessity, self-appointed, or consulted for unique expertise. The outsider perspective allows methods and observations that insiders miss, while creating friction with official channels.
entry_conditions: Investigator lacks institutional authority or official standing
exit_conditions: Resolution leverages the outsider perspective as a specific advantage that insiders could not have achieved
typical_variants: Consulting genius called in by police baffled by the case
Amateur with relevant specialized knowledge
Civilian drawn into investigation by personal connection
Retired professional with unofficial expertise
failure_modes: Outsider status creates no meaningful friction or advantage
Amateur investigates with full institutional resources despite lack of authority
Outsider perspective adds color but not substance to the solution
signals_in_text: Detective's lack of authority creates specific obstacles
Official investigators are wrong in ways only the outsider can see
Unconventional methods or observations provide the key insight
Tension between official and unofficial investigation is dramatized

nodes.label: Crime Scene as Evidence Field
nodes.definition: Physical spaces in the detective story must function as evidence fields — environments that contain readable information about what happened. The crime scene is not merely a backdrop but an active element of the investigation, yielding clues through careful observation and analysis.
entry_conditions: A crime or central mystery has occurred
exit_conditions: Physical evidence from the crime scene contributes meaningfully to the solution
typical_variants: Traditional crime scene with physical trace evidence
Body and surroundings that reveal cause and timeline
Scene of disappearance or unexplained event
Secondary locations that extend the evidence trail
failure_modes: Crime scene is visited but yields no meaningful evidence
Physical setting is irrelevant to the solution
Evidence is only provided through witness testimony, never physical observation
signals_in_text: Detective moves through space with deliberate, observational attention
Details of the environment are noted by the investigator and prove significant
Scene reconstruction — what happened here — is dramatized through the detective's analysis

nodes.label: Interview and Interrogation Dynamics
nodes.definition: The social world of the detective story is populated by people who conceal and reveal information through conversation — witnesses who misremember, suspects who lie, bystanders who know more than they think. The detective's skill in reading and questioning people is as important as reading physical evidence.
entry_conditions: Multiple characters with knowledge of the case exist
exit_conditions: At least one crucial piece of information is obtained through the detective's skill in human interaction
typical_variants: Formal police interrogation testing a suspect's consistency
Informal conversation designed to lower a subject's guard
Reading behavioral tells and body language
Strategic use of partial information to provoke revealing responses
failure_modes: Characters volunteer all relevant information without detective skill being applied
Interview scenes are expository — information dumps without tension
Detective's social skill is never tested by a genuinely resistant subject
signals_in_text: Characters attempt to conceal or redirect during questioning
Detective notices what a subject doesn't say as much as what they do say
Interview scenes have stakes — the subject's cooperation or evasion affects the case
Contradictions between accounts are identified and exploited

nodes.label: Institutional Context
nodes.definition: The detective operates within or against systems — legal, social, bureaucratic, or organizational — and these systems shape what the investigator can do, who they can pressure, and what counts as a valid solution. The institutional context is not incidental but structures the investigation's constraints and possibilities.
exit_conditions: Institutional factors — authority, access, rules, or social position — materially affect at least one significant investigative decision
typical_variants: Police procedural constraints — warrant requirements, chain of custody
Social hierarchy that protects certain suspects from scrutiny
Legal limitations on what evidence is admissible
Institutional corruption that the detective must navigate or circumvent
failure_modes: Detective operates in a social vacuum with no institutional friction
Institutional context is mentioned but never creates real constraints
Detective ignores rules without consequence, rendering the institution decorative
signals_in_text: Detective is denied access to information or locations due to authority limitations
Institutional rules force the detective to choose between the letter of procedure and the truth
Social position of suspects or witnesses affects how aggressively they can be pursued
System creates obstacles that must be worked around or through

nodes.label: Case Assignment / Discovery Scene
nodes.definition: An early scene that formally initiates the detective's engagement with the case — whether through official assignment, being hired, stumbling upon a crime, or being personally compelled to investigate. This scene establishes the mystery's stakes, the detective's relationship to the case, and the initial framing of what must be solved.
exit_conditions: Detective has accepted or committed to the case, and the initial mystery is defined
typical_variants: Client arrives at the detective's office with a problem
Investigator is officially assigned to a case by the institution
Detective discovers or witnesses the crime directly
Personal connection draws the detective into the case without formal request
failure_modes: Case begins without any scene establishing the detective's commitment to it
Initial framing of the mystery is so vague as to provide no investigative direction
Stakes are established so abstractly that the audience has no emotional investment
signals_in_text: A specific question — who did it, where is she, what happened — is placed before the detective
Detective's decision to take the case is dramatized, not skipped
Initial information about the mystery is provided, along with what remains unknown
Tone of the case is established — stakes, danger level, personal relevance

nodes.label: Investigation Breakthrough Scene
nodes.definition: A mid-story scene in which the detective achieves a key deduction, discovery, or insight that substantially changes the course of the investigation — not the final solution, but a turning-point revelation that opens new lines of inquiry or reframes the mystery in a significant way.
entry_conditions: Investigation has been underway with some struggle or misdirection
Detective has gathered sufficient evidence to support a major insight
exit_conditions: The investigation has been reoriented by the discovery, and the path to the solution is now visible even if not yet complete
typical_variants: Physical evidence discovery that reframes the timeline or method
Key witness interview that reveals a hidden relationship or motive
Logical synthesis scene where the detective connects previously disparate facts
Discovery that a prior assumption was wrong — wrong suspect, wrong motive, wrong crime
failure_modes: Breakthrough comes too easily — no prior effort makes it feel earned
Breakthrough is provided by another character rather than the detective's own work
Discovery has no meaningful impact on the investigation's direction
signals_in_text: Detective's demeanor shifts — the picture is becoming clearer
Previously gathered evidence is recontextualized by the new discovery
Investigation accelerates in a new direction after the breakthrough
Audience shares the moment of insight with the detective

nodes.label: Confrontation / Solution Presentation Scene
nodes.definition: The climactic scene in which the detective reveals the solution — naming the culprit, demonstrating the method, and closing the case. This scene must show the detective's work and reasoning, not merely announce a result, and must feel both surprising and retrospectively inevitable.
entry_conditions: Detective has gathered sufficient evidence to identify and prove the solution
All relevant suspects or parties are available to receive the revelation
exit_conditions: Case is resolved, culprit is identified and confronted, and the detective's reasoning is demonstrated
typical_variants: Classic drawing-room assembly where detective walks through the full deduction
Direct confrontation with the culprit who is exposed by the detective's evidence
Formal institutional presentation of evidence — courtroom, briefing room
Private revelation to the culprit, followed by their response
failure_modes: Solution is announced without the reasoning being demonstrated
Culprit confesses spontaneously, making the detective's work redundant
Resolution involves the detective presenting a solution that the audience cannot verify through prior scenes
Final scene feels rushed — case closed without emotional or intellectual payoff
signals_in_text: Detective walks through the chain of evidence and reasoning
Earlier scenes are retrospectively recontextualized — clues that seemed irrelevant now click into place
Culprit's response — denial, anger, confession — validates the revelation
Audience experiences the double pleasure of surprise and retrospective inevitability

nodes.label: Intellectual Rigor and Methodical Persistence
nodes.definition: The detective genre requires a pervasive sense that the world is knowable through careful attention and rigorous reasoning — that chaos can be ordered, truth can be recovered, and method can prevail over confusion. Even when the detective struggles, the tone communicates that the investigative process itself has value and dignity.
exit_conditions: Tone maintains confidence in investigative method as a value even when results are elusive
typical_variants: Cool, analytical precision in a genius detective's voice
Dogged, unglamorous persistence in a procedural detective's approach
Weary moral seriousness in a hardboiled detective's worldview
Playful intellectual confidence in a golden-age puzzle mystery
failure_modes: Investigation is presented as futile or arbitrary — method doesn't matter
Detective's approach feels like random guessing dignified as deduction
Tone is so grim or cynical that the value of solving the case is undermined
signals_in_text: Detective's reasoning is presented with care and detail
Attention to evidence — physical, testimonial, behavioral — is modeled as meaningful
Other characters recognize the investigator's method as distinct and valuable
Resolution confirms that the method worked — the case was solvable through diligence

nodes.label: Detective as Passive Observer
nodes.definition: The detective genre fails when the investigator's work is essentially passive — when the solution falls into the detective's lap through spontaneous confession, coincidental discovery, or the actions of other characters rather than through the detective's own active investigation and reasoning.
exit_conditions: Solution must arise from active investigative effort — observation, deduction, interview skill, or persistence — not from luck or others' agency
typical_variants: Culprit confesses without being cornered by the detective's evidence
Key witness volunteers crucial information unprompted
Coincidental event reveals the truth without detective action
Another character solves it and tells the detective
failure_modes: This node IS the failure mode — its presence indicates a genre violation
signals_in_text: Detective is present when revelation occurs but did not cause it
Solution speech references information not gathered through investigative scenes
Audience cannot trace a causal link from the detective's actions to the resolution
Detective seems surprised by a solution they should have been driving toward

edges.label: Promise requires a dedicated central investigator
preconditions: Genre promise established
edges.effects_on_stakes: All case stakes are filtered through the investigator's personal engagement
edges.effects_on_character: Investigator becomes the audience's primary point of identification and the engine of plot
common_alternatives: Lone investigator vs. investigator with partner vs. investigator leading a team
anti_patterns: No clear investigator — case resolved by diffuse ensemble action

edges.label: Promise requires visible investigative method
preconditions: Genre promise established
edges.effects_on_stakes: Audience engagement depends on being able to follow the detective's reasoning
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's method becomes an expression of character — their way of seeing the world
common_alternatives: Logical deduction vs. physical observation vs. procedural elimination vs. intuitive insight
anti_patterns: Method is asserted in description but never dramatized in practice

edges.label: Promise requires a genuinely challenging case
preconditions: Genre promise established
edges.effects_on_stakes: Case difficulty determines how impressive the solution feels
edges.effects_on_character: A worthy case tests the detective's limits and reveals their depth
common_alternatives: Puzzle complexity vs. moral complexity vs. investigative danger as source of challenge
anti_patterns: Trivial case resolved without meaningful effort or misdirection

edges.label: Promise requires solution earned through detective's work
preconditions: Genre promise established
edges.effects_on_stakes: Audience satisfaction depends on the detective's work being causally necessary to the resolution
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's agency and competence are validated by the earned solution
common_alternatives: Solution through evidence vs. through confrontation vs. through logical synthesis
anti_patterns: Coincidental resolution, spontaneous confession, or solution delivered by a secondary character

edges.label: Promise sets tone of intellectual rigor and persistence
preconditions: Genre promise established
edges.effects_on_stakes: Tone signals to audience that method will matter and the case is solvable
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's approach is presented as worthy of respect and capable of success
common_alternatives: Analytical precision vs. dogged persistence vs. playful puzzle-solving vs. weary moral seriousness
anti_patterns: Tone communicates that investigation is futile or that method is irrelevant to outcome

edges.label: Logical method formalized as puzzle-solving
preconditions: Method is primarily logical deduction applied to an elegantly constructed puzzle
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes are intellectual — the satisfaction of a perfectly solved puzzle
edges.effects_on_character: Detective is characterized by exceptional intellect and a superior observational gift
common_alternatives: Hardboiled
Police procedural
Amateur consulting
anti_patterns: Genius detective applied to a trivially simple puzzle
Deduction scenes that rely on information the audience was not given

edges.label: Method expressed through personal code in corrupt world
preconditions: Method is persistence and moral compass rather than pure logic
World is morally corrupt and physically dangerous
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes are moral and physical — survival and integrity in a compromised world
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's method is inseparable from their personal code of conduct under pressure
common_alternatives: Classic golden age
Police procedural
Amateur consulting
anti_patterns: Hardboiled detective in a safe, honest world
Personal code that is never genuinely tested

edges.label: Method expressed through procedural rigor within institution
preconditions: Method includes procedural discipline and institutional accountability
Investigator operates within a formal law-enforcement structure
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes include both the case outcome and the investigator's standing within the institution
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's method is shaped and constrained by institutional identity and team dynamics
common_alternatives: Classic golden age
Hardboiled
Amateur consulting
anti_patterns: Police label without procedural substance
Institutional context that never creates real constraints on the investigation

edges.label: Method expressed through outsider perspective and unconventional approach
preconditions: Investigator lacks official authority
Method derives value from outsider vantage point and specialized knowledge
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes include both the case and the detective's lack of institutional protection
edges.effects_on_character: Outsider status defines both the detective's unique insight and their specific vulnerabilities
common_alternatives: Classic golden age
Hardboiled
Police procedural
anti_patterns: Amateur detective with full institutional access and no outsider friction

edges.label: Golden-age puzzle centers on reading physical evidence
preconditions: Classic subgenre selected
edges.effects_on_stakes: Physical details of the scene carry the puzzle — each object potentially significant
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's observational genius is demonstrated through the crime scene
common_alternatives: Locked-room scenario
Poisoning case requiring chemical knowledge
Scene with time-of-death evidence as central puzzle element
anti_patterns: Golden-age puzzle with no physical evidence — testimony-only mystery

edges.label: Hardboiled investigation runs on dangerous human encounters
preconditions: Hardboiled subgenre selected
edges.effects_on_stakes: Interview scenes carry physical danger as well as informational stakes
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's ability to read and handle difficult, hostile, or dangerous people is central to survival and success
common_alternatives: Intimidation tactics
Building rapport with reluctant informants
Working criminal contacts
anti_patterns: Hardboiled detective who gets full cooperation from every subject with no friction

edges.label: Police procedural is structured by institutional rules and hierarchy
preconditions: Police procedural subgenre selected
edges.effects_on_stakes: Institutional rules constrain how the detective can pursue the case — procedural violations have consequences
edges.effects_on_character: Detective must balance personal conviction about the truth against institutional compliance
common_alternatives: Conflict with superior over methods
Bureaucratic obstruction from within the institution
Use of institutional resources that enable but also limit investigation
anti_patterns: Police detective who operates without any institutional accountability or constraint

edges.label: Amateur detective relies on conversational skill to compensate for lack of authority
preconditions: Amateur/consulting subgenre selected
edges.effects_on_stakes: Without coercive authority, detective must obtain information through social skill alone
edges.effects_on_character: Amateur's approach to interviews reveals their unconventional perspective and social intelligence
common_alternatives: Charm and apparent harmlessness as disarming tools
Specialized knowledge that makes subjects want to cooperate
Leveraging personal relationships
anti_patterns: Amateur detective who obtains all information without any social effort or skill

edges.label: Crime scene discovery initiates detective's case engagement
preconditions: Crime or mystery has occurred and a physical scene exists
edges.effects_on_stakes: Initial evidence at the scene defines the apparent nature of the case and its first misdirections
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's first encounter with the scene establishes their method in action
common_alternatives: Detective arrives at scene after being assigned
Detective is the first to discover the scene
Scene is reconstructed through witness accounts rather than direct observation
anti_patterns: Case begins without any scene establishing the crime or its initial evidence

edges.label: Key interview produces critical investigative breakthrough
preconditions: Detective has been interviewing witnesses or suspects
Investigation requires human intelligence to advance
edges.effects_on_stakes: Breakthrough from interview changes the investigation's direction and raises the stakes
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's skill in reading people is vindicated by the breakthrough
common_alternatives: Breakthrough from physical evidence rather than interview
Breakthrough from logical synthesis of multiple prior interviews
anti_patterns: All investigation advances through physical evidence — human interaction contributes nothing

edges.label: Institutional context frames how the case is assigned and defined
preconditions: Investigator operates within or against an institutional structure
edges.effects_on_stakes: Institutional framing of the case may differ from the actual truth — creating a tension to be resolved
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's relationship to the institution shapes their initial approach to the case
common_alternatives: Official assignment vs. unofficial investigation vs. consulting engagement
anti_patterns: Case assigned without any institutional context affecting how it is framed or constrained

edges.label: Case engagement drives investigation toward breakthrough
preconditions: Detective has accepted the case and begun active investigation
edges.effects_on_stakes: Breakthrough must be proportionate to the investigation's difficulty — too easy undercuts the case's worthiness
edges.effects_on_character: The struggle before the breakthrough reveals the detective's persistence and adaptability
common_alternatives: Single major breakthrough vs. series of smaller revelations accumulating to the solution
anti_patterns: Breakthrough arrives without prior investigative work that makes it feel earned

edges.label: Investigation breakthrough leads to solution presentation
preconditions: Breakthrough has reoriented the investigation
Detective now has sufficient evidence to identify and confront the solution
edges.effects_on_stakes: Solution presentation must deliver on the breakthrough's promise — the reframing must fully illuminate the crime
edges.effects_on_character: Detective moves from investigator to revealer — their accumulated understanding is finally shared
common_alternatives: Immediate confrontation after breakthrough vs. further investigation before final presentation
anti_patterns: Breakthrough leads nowhere — solution presentation ignores what the breakthrough revealed

edges.label: Case difficulty makes breakthrough meaningful
preconditions: Case has presented genuine obstacles and misdirection
edges.effects_on_stakes: Difficulty of the case proportionally increases the value of the breakthrough
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's breakthrough is earned through demonstrated effort against genuine resistance
common_alternatives: Difficulty expressed through false leads vs. through active antagonist obstruction vs. through complexity of evidence
anti_patterns: Easy case where breakthrough requires no real insight or effort

edges.label: Solution earned through detective's work is demonstrated in presentation
preconditions: Solution has been arrived at through the detective's active investigation
edges.effects_on_stakes: Presentation must make the detective's causal role in the solution visible and verifiable
edges.effects_on_character: Detective's competence and method are validated through the demonstrated reasoning
common_alternatives: Full reconstruction of chain of evidence vs. focused revelation of key deduction vs. direct confrontation with culprit
anti_patterns: Solution announced without the detective's reasoning being demonstrated — audience cannot verify the work

edges.label: Unearned solution violates the detective contract
preconditions: Solution arises without being caused by detective's investigative work
edges.effects_on_stakes: Audience feels cheated — the promise of watching a detective solve a case is unfulfilled
edges.effects_on_character: Detective is rendered decorative — their presence is incidental to the outcome
anti_patterns: This edge IS the anti-pattern — it describes what to avoid

edges.label: Classic puzzle focus differs from hardboiled moral danger
preconditions: Both subtypes apply core constraints but diverge in emphasis
edges.effects_on_stakes: Classic stakes are intellectual; hardboiled stakes are moral and physical
edges.effects_on_character: Classic detective is elevated by superior intellect; hardboiled detective is defined by moral endurance under pressure
common_alternatives: Hybrid works that blend puzzle elegance with moral complexity
anti_patterns: Classic puzzle applied to a genuinely dangerous, morally corrupt world without adjustment
Hardboiled tone without any real danger or moral compromise

edges.label: Institutional authority differs from outsider perspective
preconditions: Both subtypes apply core constraints but derive their distinctive advantages from opposite positions
edges.effects_on_stakes: Police detective risks institutional standing; amateur detective risks personal safety with no institutional protection
edges.effects_on_character: Police detective navigates authority and compliance; amateur detective leverages unconventional thinking free from institutional assumptions
common_alternatives: Consulting arrangement where outsider works within institutional framework — hybrid of both
anti_patterns: Police detective with no institutional accountability
Amateur detective with full institutional resources and authority
